MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Dec-22-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF HELLO NEW LEGACY, LLC, DOING BUSINESS AS, EUN KIM MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication is denied.

On summary judgment, "the rule continues that the moving party's evidence must be strictly construed, while the opposing party's evidence must be liberally construed." (Binder v. Aetna Lie Ins. Co. (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 832, 838.)

There are a triable issues of material fact regarding whether plaintiffs substantially performed their agreements. (Magic Carpet Ride LLC v. Rugger Investment Group, L.L.C. (2019) 41 Cal.App.5th 357, 364 ["What constitutes substantial performance is a question of fact."].) The evidence creating triable issues of material fact are as follows: cause of action one (Kim Decl., par. 15; Robinson Decl., 3:1-8); cause of action two (Kim Decl., par. 19; Robinson Decl., 3:21-25; 4:4-5); and cause of action three (Kim Decl., par. 24; Robinson Decl., 4:4-9).

Plaintiff's objections to the above-cited evidence are overruled. The court further notes that the cited deposition testimony of Robinson and her declaration are not clearly contradictory. Therefore, an objection based on D'Amico v. Board of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal.3d 1, 21-22 is overruled.

Plaintiffs' citations to Terry v. Sickles (1859) 13 Cal. 427, 429 and Steefel, Levitt & Weiss v. Astor Holdings, Inc. (N.D. Cal. May 31, 2005) 2005 WL 8166270, at *9-11 are not persuasive as plaintiffs do not plead claims for "account stated." (Zinn v. Fred R. Bright Co. (1969) 271 Cal.App.2d 597, 600 [providing elements for cause of action for "account stated"].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/CEK)
